rationale: This live-action UGC dancing reaction hook shows zero gameplay, which is a major friction for a physics puzzle that sells on satisfying chain-reaction visuals — the core loop's "aha" moment is entirely absent in the first 3s. The dancing young-male talent also skews younger and more hype-driven than Bounce Blast 3D's 25-55 calm/relaxing-puzzle audience, and the overlay even references a different game (Crossword Go), suggesting this archetype isn't proven for physics-block titles. Only consider testing if paired with a hard cut to gameplay by second 3 and re-cast with a more age-appropriate creator; otherwise skip.
